entry.title: Creation of a generic address report at embedded People Analytics
entry.content:
Summary

The format of addresses in EC is highly country specific. This provides a high level of flexibility when showing addresses in the employee profile. However, this can also lead to challenges in reporting. In particular it is difficult to show addresses of employees for different countries in a simple list report. The blogpost explains how such a simple address report could be realized but explains also the challenges and limitations which are related to that.

Introduction

The format of addresses is highly country specific. E.g. in many countries the first address line has the street with the house number while the 2nd address line contains information about ZIP code and city. Stair/Floor/Apartment and the 3rd address line has something like district. However, in other countries it is different. In fact, there are small deviations from country to country, while there are even legal regulations. For example:

For Germany                                 For USA                                         For Australia
ALEX MÜLLER                                BEN ZIMMERMANN                       CHANDLER J GILBERT
Himmelstraße 123                          PO BOX 752                                   2089 Baker St
98760 Dresden                               Philadelphia 10011                        Sydney NSW 2108
Sachsen                                          Pennsylvania                                  New South Wales
Germany                                         USA                                                Australia

In SFSF Employee Central exists a comprehensive configuration capability for this:

  • For each country the respective address fields can be maintained. This happens under the Admin Centre activity “Manage Business Configuration”. Note that there are 25 address line fields available (address1, …. Address 25).
  • Furthermore, it is possible to maintain a set of address type, e.g. ‘business’, ‘home’, ‘vacation’ and others (the ‘address type’ itself is configurable). This means, each employee could theoretically have address information in multiple countries; e.g. business address in Switzerland and home address in Germany.

Figure 1   Two examples of the configuration of country specific address objects (left: USA, right: Russia)

This highly flexible address configuration is a challenge in reporting. Even if one company is only located in a single country, it is at least possible that the addresses of the employees cover multiple country specific address formats.

 

Challenges in Address Reporting

1. One way to get rid of that is to join many country-specific address objects as indicated in Figure 2. However, imagine you join just 20 country specific address objects (out of existing 80 existing country specific address objects) with 5 fields each, then you can a list output of more than 100 fields which is of not easy to handle.

Note that the original EC database object “Address” which connects the “Biographical Information” and the country specific Address objects is so-to-say the parent address objects; which, however, does not have any of the fields address1, …. Address 25.

Figure 2   Schematic illustration of the parent-child-relationship of the generic address object and the respective country specific children

2. Alternatively, for small customers it might be an acceptable approach to have one address report for each country (under the prerequisite that the respective employees maintain addresses only in this single address format). Example: An US company has only legal entities in USA and the employees maintain only US specific addresses.

3. However, for multinational companies which would like to have address lists for larger parts of their whole workforce in a single list output, such a workaround is not feasible. Here, a more comprehensive approach is to “merge” all country specific address objects into a single object “Address”.

Employee

Address Type

Address Line 1

Address
Line 2

Address Line 3

State

Country

ALEX MÜLLER

Business 

Himmelstraße 123

98760 Dresden

 

Sachsen

Germany

CLAIRE TONKIN

Home

PO BOX 752

Philadelphia 10011 

 

Pennsylvania

USA

CHANDLER J GILBERT

Business

2089 Baker St

Sydney NSW 2108

 

New South Wales

Australia

….

 

 

 

 

 

 

The big advantage of this approach is to avoid the usage of all the country specific address objects in the query. However, note that the downside of the approach is that it is not possible to know which information comes e.g., in “Address Line 1” for a particular employee, i.e., if it’s e.g. the street with the house number or the ZIP code in combination with the city.

In the following, the approach (3) is described more in detail; including the challenges which are associated with it.

 

Usage of object Address

In both, (1) Canvas Reporting and (2) Story Reporting, the fields address1 … address25 of object “address” are generated if the respective field is configured at least for a single country. For example,

  • If address18 is configured for Russia (in fact, this is the case in the SAP’s default configuration, it’s the so-called Russian “Region Type”), then a field “address18” is also generated in object “Address Information”
  • If address19 is configured for no country, then also no field “address19” is generated in “Address Information”

So far, so good. However, the challenge comes with fields of the country specific address objects which have a picklist. An important example is the field “State” which is typically configured with a picklist like e.g. USA, India, Australia and almost all European countries. However, there are also countries where no picklist for the state is defined, e.g. countries as Gabon and Congo.

Canvas Reporting

There are different (1) generic (= country independent) address objects and (2) country specific child address objects per address type (e.g. address type = ‘home’, ‘business’, ‘mailing’, etc.) as indicated in figure 3.

Figure 3   Report schema of an address report in canvas reporting

Note that the address objects are generated if there exists at least a single employee for the respective address type. For example,

  • If there is just a single employee e.g. with address type “Benefits” for country “Spain”, then an address object “Addresses (Benefits)” with one child object “homeAddess (Spain)” is generated.
  • If address type “Mailing” is maintained as value for picklist “address type”; however, there is no single employee with maintained data for address type “Mailing”, then also no address object “Addresses (Mailing)” is generated.

The field ‘state’ of the generic (= country independent) address object filled either with picklist label of the country specific ‘state’ (when a picklist is maintained for the field ‘state’ of the respective country specific address object) or with the string (when string is used for the field ‘state’ of the respective country specific address object).

For illustration see the example in Figure 4: While the field ‘state’ of country specific address object for Oman is configured as string, ‘state’ of the country specific address object for USA is configured as picklist. Nevertheless, the field ‘state’ from the generic address objects (“State (Mailing, label)” and “State (Benefits, label)”) also reports the expected label.

Figure 4   Sample output of an address report in canvas reporting

The prerequisite that this is working fine is that the field 'state' is configured as "Enabled = Yes" (see Figure 5). However, it doesn't matter if field 'state' is configured as data type = picklist or string; in both cases the reporting will work properly. 

Figure 5   Configuration of (generic) address object homeAddress

 

Story Reporting

The concept at story reporting is slightly different. A learning from canvas reporting was that the big amount of objects (i.e. a generic address object per address type and for each of them also country specific children) was causing confusion. Therefore it was a conscious decision in story reporting to  have only one (generic) address object (see Figure 6 for illustration).

Figure 6   Report schema of an address report in story reporting

Up to 2405 we were facing the problem that the field ‘state’ of the (generic) address object was only reported with the option ID of the picklist. From patch 13 (2405) this shortcoming was corrected, i.e. irrespective if a picklist is used as data type for the field ‘state’ of the country specific address or just a string, the field ‘state’ of (generic) address object is filled with the label.

See the example in Figure 7: While the field ‘state’ of country specific address object for Gabun (GAB) and Congo (COG) is configured as string (shown in red), ‘state’ of the country specific address object for USA and India (IND) is configured as picklist (shown in violet). However, after implementation of patch 13 (2405), the field ‘state’ from the generic address object (column “State (Home Address)”) also reports the expected label.

Figure 7   Sample output of an address report in story reporting

There are the following prerequisites that this works properly:

  1. It is required that the field 'state' of the (generic) address object is enabled and configured as “picklist”. It is just required that a picklist is assigned, however, the respective picklist values are not used; i.e. the assigned picklist can exist without picklist values.
  2. There is currently the limitation that the ‘state’ of the (generic) address object tables is not shown properly in tables of type “non-aggregated list”-table (and also not in the query preview). This means it’s mandatory to use tables of type “Cross—tab” when showing the address data in a table format.
  3. When (1) and (2) are fulfilled, it is possible to create new stories with field 'state' of the (generic) address object. To enable the proper working of the field 'state' of the (generic) address object for existing stories, it is mandatory to trigger a change of the query (it is sufficient just to navigate to the query and to save the query when navigating back to the story). In addition, it is required (1) to convert the table into a list table and afterwards back into a cross-tab or (2) to convert the story to ‘Optimized Design Experience/Optimized View Mode’.

Furthermore, it is important to emphasize that existing canvas and story reports will continue to work as before when the patch is implemented. In particular, the existing canvas and story reports will continue to work also when the data type of the (generic) address object from string to picklist is changed.

entry.title: Redis on SAP BTP - Update instance configuration parameters via CF Command Line Interface
entry.content:
Learn how to update instance configuration parameters via Cloud Foundry (CF) Command Line Interface.

To install the CF client tool check this blog post step 1 "Install the command line client for Cloud Foundry".

You can change configuration parameters of Redis instances without the need to delete and create a new instance, but you need to be aware of:

  • ⚠️  Not all configuration parameters can be updated at once. Is possible that some parameters need to be updated first then others.
  • ⚠️  Some parameters update can create some disruption of the service (downtime) due to the need of the restart of the service instance.

Please check the list of supported parameters at: https://help.sap.com/docs/redis-hyperscaler-option/redis-on-sap-btp-hyperscaler-option/parameters

Updating parameters can be done via 2 different ways:

  1. Via SAP BTP Cockpit update option
  2. Using Cloud Foundry command line interface (CLI)

In this blog we will focus on option 2.

Please refer to the Cloud Foundry CLI Reference Guide to check all available commands and their usage, also to the service documentation for some examples of using CF CLI for provisioning operations.

Follow the below steps:

1️⃣  Retrieve the instance parameters.

Logon to Cloud Foundry Environment via ‘cf login’ using the API endpoint.

cf service redis_standard_2GB_how_to --guid 

Retrieve the instance ID with ‘cf service --guid’, use then the ID to retrieve the instance parameters value using ‘cf curl /parameters’.

cf curl /v2/service_instances/81aaeacf-b8db-4f95-9392-924beb8d4864/parameters

 2️⃣  Update the selected parameter using ‘cf update-service’

After checking the parameters values we are going to update the memory using the ‘cf update-service’ command with ‘-c’ option.

USAGE: cf update-service SERVICE_INSTANCE [-p NEW_PLAN] [-c PARAMETERS_AS_JSON] [-t TAGS]

Run the below command to apply the update from 2GB to 4GB.

cf update-service redis_standard_2GB_how_to -c '{"memory":4}'

 3️⃣  Check the parameter successful update 

You can check the success of the update operation using ‘cf curl /parameters’ command to retrieve the parameters or by going to the BTP Cockpit and check the instance parameters:

⚠️Warning: you need to have the proper entitlements to be able to change parameters that will consume +memory units or for updating the service plan.

See another example of a successful update operation, by updating 2 parameters, at once.

In this example even we are passing 3 parameters we will update only 2 as duration will not change because has the same value. We can send only the ones that we need to update, no need to send the full parameters list in the JSON block.

'{maintenance_window”:
{"day_of_week": "Monday",
"duration":5,
"start_hour_utc":1}
}'

 If you want to learn more about the service check also the 'How to start using the Redis service' blog series

  1. Redis on SAP BTP - Understanding service entitlements and metrics
  2. Redis on SAP BTP - Create a service instance in CF via BTP Cockpit
  3. Redis on SAP BTP - Connect to the instance from your local PC via SSH tunnel
  4. Redis on SAP BTP - Populate a dataset to Redis cache instance using redis-cli data import
  5. Redis on SAP BTP - Update instance configuration parameters via CF Command Line Interface

Please refer always to the product official documentation at SAP Help Portal.

Thanks for your reading,

SAP BTP Backing Services – Product Management

entry.title: Re: XAPI Support for SF Learning is coming - what will be...
entry.content: xAPI is now on the roadmap for Q4/2024: https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=67837800100800006334&range=CURRENT-LAST#;INNO=000D3AAC9DD21EECA1B8B63AB034C0A3

entry.title: Re: Error 'ABAP program lines are wider than the internal...
entry.content: There are NOT many characters in any lines .

entry.title: Re: Submissions for "SAP HANA Cloud multi-model" developer challenge: Week 4 - Geospatial
Assignment Profiles in Learning - Preview
entry.content: When building an Assignment Profile in Learning, is there a way to alter the preview report to be able to view all the fields/attributes available of the learner?  For example, if I build an AP and download the preview - the State field may be blank.  Yet, we can program the AP by state.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business bydesign supplier invoice won't post into open period
entry.content:
Hello,

There is no way to automatically change the posting date of the invoice, however instead of withdrawing from approval you can approve the document and then post this to another period in the General Ledger - Source Documents view.

entry.title: What is the size limit of parameters in parameterized statements sent to the HANA client?
entry.content:
Hi, I discovered that there might be a size limit to parameters in general for parameterized statements sent to the HANA client via the node JS library.

I found a case where a very large JSON statement got truncated when sent to the HANA client and thus the SQL execution failed. I would like to determine what this size limit is for parameterized statements sent to the HANA client so that I can add a check for JSON params that exceed this limit.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business bydesign supplier invoice won't post int...
entry.content:
Hello,

There is no way to automatically change the posting date of the invoice, however instead of withdrawing from approval you can approve the document and then post this to another period in the General Ledger - Source Documents view.

Hope this can help.

entry.title: Re: Bem vindo ao grupo da Comunidade SAP do PORTO (Portugal)
entry.content: Nem mais!  Bem pontuado!

entry.title: Re: Error while calling SAP API Proxy connected to SAP Gateway service
entry.content: Hi Jovita, there are not any specific issues associated with the error you listed. It typically indicates an issue with communicating with the backend (ERP). This can be because the backend is overloaded, misconfigured, temporarily unresponsive, sending a malformed response or any other host of reasons. I would recommend opening a trace session with APIM to see if there is any misconfiguration within the tooling, additionally the backend should be debugged to see if any issue is occurring in the response generation if nothing is found in the tool. If the issue is intermittent it is extremely hard to isolate a communication/configuration error.

entry.title: About SAP S/4HANA Certification.
entry.content:
A certification in S/4 HANA can bring several benefits to your resume, self-esteem, and professional qualifications.

Benefits of the CV:

  1. Professional Appreciation: Certification officially validates your skills and knowledge in S/4 HANA, making your resume more attractive to employers.
  2. Differentiation in the Market: You stand out from the competition with a recognized certification.
  3. Greater Employability: Companies are constantly looking for professionals qualified in S/4 HANA, and having this certification significantly increases your employment opportunities anywhere in the world!

Benefits for Self-Esteem:

  1. Recognition of Competency: Certification means you have advanced knowledge and skills in S/4 HANA, which can increase your professional confidence.
  2. Personal Satisfaction: Completing a challenging certification brings a sense of personal accomplishment and satisfaction, raising your self-esteem.
  3. Motivation: Achieving a certification can serve as motivation to seek even more knowledge and career growth.

Benefits for Professional Qualifications:

  1. Advanced Knowledge: Certification provides a deep understanding of financial processes in S/4 HANA, improving your ability to solve complex problems.
  2. Technological Update: As S/4 HANA is one of SAP's most advanced ERP platforms, certification ensures you are up to date with the latest technologies.
  3. Network: By obtaining certification, you enter a community of certified professionals, which can lead to new networking and collaboration opportunities.
  4. Salary Increase: Certified professionals generally have the potential to receive higher salaries than those without certification.
  5. Career Progression: Certification can open doors to higher positions within your organization or other companies.

Some important steps to prepare for certification:

  1. Have a system with the SAP version you intend to carry out certification with available. In this case, if you don't have one in your company, you can subscribe to a platform such as https://grandesprojetos.com/, which provides SAP courses and offers SAP in different versions. You can subscribe to SAP and access it without purchasing additional courses. However, depending on your level of knowledge, some additional courses may be interesting.
  2. Subscribe to the OpenSAP platform for SAP courses developed by SAP itself. The OpenSAP platform will be discontinued in July 2024, and all its content will be on the SAP Learning website (https://learning.sap.com/opensap-course-migration). This new platform also offers many free SAP courses.
  3. Subscribe to platforms with simulated SAP certification exams; as a suggestion, the website http://www.erpprep.com has good simulations.
  4. Try to do one or two mock tests every day until the exam date. Many questions will be repeated, and you will be increasingly able to answer immediately and without hesitation.

Conclusion:

Investing in an S/4 HANA certification can be a significant step in your career development, providing recognition, knowledge, and opportunities for personal and professional growth.

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP: Execute Business Object Interface from action
entry.content: Thanks for the response Andre.

entry.title: Re: Error while calling SAP API Proxy connected to SAP Gateway service
entry.content: You would find the error logs in /IWFND/ERROR_LOG if there is an error directly with the gateway service. Connectivity related issues may result in log entries found elsewhere... e.g. SM21, thread traces, ICM logs, etc.

entry.title: Re: {Base Table} -> {Views} Linkages
entry.content:
I used "SYS"."OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES" and passed the table to get list of Views using this table directly/indirectly. But i need a way to map the view/field to table/field mainly for Calc Views and Hierarchy Views.

I used "SYS"."CS_VIEW_COLUMNS" but it does not cover all the views. So may be i might need to query multiple views to get me the complete data set.

Any pointers will be helpful.

entry.title: Re: I have added custom object under multiprovider but it's not showing up in BEx query designer
entry.content: My questions is in 1st pic "Corp FPA account" and "Corp FPA Profitcenter" shows up under the multiprovider (Reporting) then why it is not showing up the same structure in Bex query designer.

entry.title: Re: Error 'ABAP program lines are wider than the internal table' not showing in other SAP systems
entry.content: It should not be difficult to find the reason because this error happens READ REPORT into an internal table whose width is too short for the read program, as explained in the ABAP documentation, so just add a breakpoint at READ REPORT and you'll get the exact reason in a matter of minutes.

entry.title: Appearing Custom field inputs in MIRO item level (BAdI mrm_item_custfields)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I apologize if I have not followed other thread helps and the question is repeated. 

I followed the below thread to implement the custom tab in the MIRO item level except I left or not done the append fields to RSEG table. Remaining of all the steps provided in the link are covered. 

https://www.sapyard.com/steps-to-add-custom-tab-in-standard-miro-item-screen-using-badi-mrm_item_custfields/

Other than my screen inputs all other values are appearing (in t_drseg_custom TYPE tdrseg_cust ). I can see my fields are there in the debugging screen but values are not passed. 

Please help me  where did I miss and how to fix this? Many thanks. 

Note:

The reason I left the RSEG part , I have a different requirement where I have to use the Fiori custom fields, which are already created in RSEG, that are to used to for saving the values. A different thread (Q&A) has been posted and waiting for response/answers. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/miro-badi-using-calling-fiori-custom-fields-in-drseg-ci-and-ci-drseg-cust/qaq-p/13742454

I would be grateful if anyone address to the above as well.

Thanks for your time here.

entry.title: Re: bdc
entry.content:
Hi,

Did you get the explanation of these ok codes? If so, please explain

entry.title: Re: PO Currency based on Company Code
entry.content: you can create a condition record in MEK2 with the material company code (if you don't have this condition table with this combination, you can create one) and save it , afterwards you can delete this condition record, now system will determine the company code currency in PO and info records price will automatically converts to company code currency

entry.title: Re: License heartbeat not reading the license key
entry.content:
I ran the following test:

  • removed the EE/EV license file
  • modified the sysam/properties file with EE/AC (invalid in my case since I don't have an AC license)
  • bounced the ASE instance (ASE 16.0 SP04 PL04)
  • errorlog says can't find license file but allows ASE to start on graced EE/EV license
  • sp_lmconfig shows EE/AC (per sysam/properties file) and the EE/EV license as graced
  • reinstalled the EE/EV license file
  • 2 hrs later the SySAM license heartbeat sees the EE/EV license file and prints several lines into the ASE errorlog (in this case stating the EE/EV licenses are going to expire in September)
  • sp_lmconfig still shows the invalid EE/AC (per sysam/properties file) but the EE/EV licenses are now showing expirable

Net result: the SySAM license heartbeat process appears to behave as expected (in my environment)

I don't have a GCP environment nor an OEM/HOSTID=ANY license to test with so I've run out of ideas.

If I understand your latest comment correctly ... ASE does see the license upon reboot of the ASE instance.  If this is correct but you're still interested in knowing why you have to bounce the ASE instance then all I can suggest is to open a case with tech support ... perhaps there's an issue with the GCP + OEM/HOSTID=ANY combo?

entry.title: Re: What is the size limit of parameters in parameterized...
entry.content:
As far as I know, there is no limit to the size of parameters in the @Sap/hana_client Node.js driver.  For large LOB parameters (say parameters over 1MB), by using Statement.sendParameterData you will reduce the amount of memory required to send a large LOB parameter.  But either by passing the parameter directly to an execute method or using Statement.sendParameterData I am not aware of a maximum size limit.

Which node.js driver are you using?

Kind regards,

Ian

entry.title: Re: Showing Partial Dimension Members in SAC Chart Using...
entry.content:
Hi,

This partial dimension members can be derived using transformations at Model level or Calculated dimension at Widget level. That makes your requirement easier than using scripting.

Thanks,

Venkat

entry.title: Re: Submissions for "SAP HANA Cloud multi-model" developer challenge: Week 4 - Geospatial
entry.content:
My submission,

From Vitoria (Spain) to Denpasar (Indonesia) in 4 hops.

congrats @Vitaliy-R!, I have participad in several challenge but this is one of the best!

entry.title: Re: Integration of custom fields into Standard Reports dynamic selections
entry.content: Great!  Thanks for sharing!

entry.title: Re: Zipping multiple files from SFTP to SFTP in one folder
entry.content:
Hi Shreyas,

Please understand that the SFTP adapter processes only one file per message. In my scenario, for example, I needed to fetch fileWithHeaderInfo.csv and fileWithDetailsInfo.csv, so instead of reading all the files in the directory, I had two RECEIVER SFTP polling for specific files.

Please find one of the subprocesses used to fetch the file from the SFTP server (please ignore the Decrypt subprocess - the files were PGP encrypted)

Note: Another subprocess similar to this was used to fetch the second file. In a variant of this iFlow with more files (fileA.csv, fileB.csv, fileC.csv), I simply used the iterator step and triggered the poll multiple times for each file.

Regards 🖖🏻

entry.title: Re: Blocking of Sales Order thru SP Transaction Notification
entry.content:
Try this

IF(@object_type = '17') and (@transaction_type in ('A', 'U'))

BEGIN

DECLARE @Customer NVARCHAR(50)
DECLARE @check INT
SET @Customer = (SELECT CardCode FROM OINV T0 WHERE T0.DocEntry = @List_of_cols_val_tab_del)
SET @check = (SELECT COUNT(T0.Docentry) FROM OINV T0 WHERE T0.CardCode = @Customer AND T0.[DocStatus] = 'O' AND T0.CANCELED='N' )
IF @check > 1 
    BEGIN
    SET @error = 1001
    SET @error_message = 'You have Pending AR invoice Open, Please Settle Customer Account. Thank you!'
    END
END

Cheers Andy

entry.title: Re: Error during reusing an article after discontinue
entry.content: Could you tell me step by step how you did it, it won't let me change the information

entry.title: Re: Help with formula
entry.content:
Thank you so much Dan for wanting to help me.
What I want to do is connect a Google sheet via rest API to Soft tabs. The connection with rest API works well, but want to connect it to a table and control it via Soft tabs
To give you more understanding about what I want to do, I can send you an example as a gpg-fill.
Where can I send it?

entry.title: Re: Get the latest information about the new Integrated Learning Experience in SAP SuccessFactors

entry.title: Re: Get to know what’s new for SAP SuccessFactors Learning with 1H2024 release
entry.content:
@MargitBauer Can you please provide the links to the documentation "transition guide" and "rules"?  I cannot locate them.  Also in a previous version of the transition guide, the various links embedded in the PDF to are not working, so hopefully the links in the transition guide you provide, are working.

Thank you!

entry.title: Third Party dropship item category for Brazil (BANC products)
entry.content:
Hi ,

Standard SAP has a flow for TA orders and BANC products, that is Standard SAP 3rd party scenario with material defined as BANC-Individual order (Item category group from material master) , described in

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/7b24a64d9d0941bda1afa753263d9e39/5166b65334e6b54ce10000000a174cb4.html

Looking at ORB Standand order type for Brazil ,  BANC item category group are not defined ? Is that normal or are we missing something standard SAP for this flow ? Any oss note , so the ORB flow can be done for BANC item category group  materials ?

Thank you .

entry.title: BW/4HANA Cockpit slowness
entry.content:
Hi guys.

Our basis team has implemted the BW/4HANA Cockpit, however, every time we try to managed any object, the cockpit does not show any tile.

Does any body knows how to solved this or is there any workaround.

BR Oscar

entry.title: Re: CPI with PostgreSQL
entry.content:
@ArielBravo 

The payload you attached, actually not leads to SQL injection. In direct table update cases it will . I am doing through stored functions and hope it will be not there on that. Kindly let me know if there is still loop hole on this

And even valid SQL query is passed onto function, it will be treated as string not actual query..

( Attaching Screenshot for your ref )

Direct table insert

 

Through Function

entry.title: Re: How to set default filter when we choose GL Account
entry.content:
Hi Ariel,

I tried to set a default filter but I got the same result since it is not available yet.

That said, it is possible to set an account if the user is selecting a material through the “Automatic Account Determination” (SSCUI 100297) using the valuation class of the material (please see screenshots below).

If no material is used you could maintain the same SSCUI leaving an empty Valuation Class

I hope this helps.

Ernesto Cepeda

entry.title: In CIDS How do you MERGE 2 INPUT files into 1 OUTPUT File
entry.content: I have 2 files created from SAP tables, MARD and MSKA, that have the same format. I need to MERGE these to files into 1 combined file. INPUT1 has 10 records...INPUT2 has 15...my OUTPUT needs to contain 25 records. Is easy to do in Data Services with the MERGE Transform but in CIDS I see no way.

entry.title: Re: Last Invoice Date in List of Customer Business Partners
entry.content: Thanks for the tip! I finally managed to see the Last Invoice Date appear in Customer BP Master Data but when select from OCRD UDF value is empty?

entry.title: Re: FAGL_FCV - Back Dated Postings at Month end
entry.content:
Hi Andre,

Good day.

Have you resolved this case? if yes, please share the resolution/workaround for this scenario. Greatly appreciate it. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: What is the size limit of parameters in parameterized...
entry.content:
Hi Ian,

Thanks for the quick response. I will check out Statement.sendParameterData and see if that gives me the same error. 

I am using node version 18.18.2 and @Sap/hana-client version 2.15.19

$ npm list @Sap/hana-client
wrangling@2024.14.3 C:\SAPDevelop\wrangling
├── @Sap/hana-client@2.15.19
├─┬ @Sap/orca-starter-datarepository@2.63.0 invalid: "3.7.0" from the root project
│ └─┬ @Sap/orca-starter-data-hana@2.63.0
│ └── @Sap/hana-client@2.16.26
└─┬ db-migrate-hana@1.3.7 -> .\db-migrate-hana
└── @Sap/hana-client@2.15.19 deduped

Please let me know if you have any further insight.

Thanks,

Soumya

entry.title: Re: solman_setup - unable to execute activity Database Extractor Setup
entry.content: OK, I can create... But I would rather guess that the problem is with the managed system. Why with the Solution Manager ? When other satellite systems do not have this problem ?

entry.title: Re: SAP Solman Integration with ServiceNow
entry.content: Hello, I processed according to note 2556872 and it looks promising. Can you please confirm, that this will create tickets in ServiceNow when the System Monitoring generates alerts ? Many thanks!

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Stock Room Management
entry.content: @bruno_bazzoli2 , Functionally, the differences between LE-WM and Stock Room Management are in the functionality that is considered out of scope for Stock Room, which is described in the FAQ note, 2881166 (https://me.sap.com/notes/2881166).  Otherwise, they are effectively one and the same (same transaction codes, tables, etc. in an S/4 context).

entry.title: Re: SAP Solman Integration with ServiceNow
entry.content: Hello, what is the name of this standard web service please ?

entry.title: Re: How to Mass update Classification data
entry.content:
Hello , 

thanks for your reply but i want to mass change the fields . not to export ....

Can you help me to do this ?

entry.title: Re: Step by Step Process of Integrating SAP Solution Manager to External Systems
entry.content:
Hello,

is it a prerequisite to have configured Service Desk in the Solution Manager ?
We are looking for a way to automatically create tickets in ServiceNow for Alarms from System Monitoring in Solution Manager. What would be the easiest way to achieve that ? Many thanks!

entry.title: Re: SAP BO - What are the possible reasons for 'no data to retrieve' in a webi report?
entry.content: Only one reason. The SQL generated by your web intelligence query does not return any data. Copy the SQL and paste it into your native database query tool anf troubleshoot from there

entry.title: Re: Anatomy of a Function Module: POPUP_TO_CONFIRM – Part 4
entry.content: Great content! I enjoyed reading these blog posts and the easiness of the explanations! Is it possible to link all the blogs together to jump between each other? At first I didn't know there were 4 parts until I google them.

entry.title: Mapping Object Entity to ONB2DataCollectionUserConfig Object
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm having an issue of where the existing association configured between a created onboarding custom data collection object and the ONB2DataCollectionUserConfig object using the "Join By Column" as per link Mapping Object Entity to ONB2DataCollectionUserConfig Object | SAP Help Portal is not being picked up.

So, what happens is that the onboardee will successfully complete the additional data collection step however when you check the onboardees data under manage data> Onboarding data collection user configuration, the data for additional custom data collection object does not exist. However, the data as captured by the onboardee does exist under Manage Data with it's own external code.

I have subsequently tried to re-create the association using the "Join By Column" however with the latest release updates, I get the error message "Type “Join By Column” is not supported on association xxxxxx. Supported types are: Composite,Valid When" when trying to save the Join By Column association.  

I have also tried changing the association to "valid when" however the association is still not being created hence I'm unable to map the data from the custom object to the custom form for signing.

Has anyone come across this and been able to successfully create the association for the additional custom data collection object to the ONB2DataCollectionConfig object?

Regards

Jackie

entry.title: About using SAP Companion Guided Tours for WebGUI Screens in SAP S4
entry.content:
As we know, SAP S4 user interface can be experienced primarily through UI5 apps (Fiori apps), and WebGUI screens. WebGUI mimics the classic SAP GUI experience, and accessible through Web Browser.

So, while authoring SAP Companion Guided Tours for the SAP S4 application, we found two types of screens - UI5 Fiori Screens, and WebGUI screens.

We have not encountered any challenges while authoring guided tours for UI5 Fiori screens. However, when we authored guided tours for WebGUI screens in SAP S4, we found the following issues:

  1. The guided tours do not function consistently for all browsers.
  2. The positioning of bubbles and highlights changes sometimes.
  3. When a user moves from WebGUI screen to another, a bubble gets repeated.

When raised a question to SAP, I was also suggested to turn on the Scripting. For the Scripting feature, I was provided with this blog on SAP Community.

Additionally, I was recommended to ask this Question on SAP Community. Hence, I am adding it here as well. So, any help would be greatly appreciated.

My questions:

  • Anyone encountered behaviors as listed above in points # 1, 2, and 3 while authoring SAP Companion content for WebGUI screens of SAP S4 application?
  • How to turn on the Scripting? Is it done in the target SAP S4 application, or in SAP GUI at the back-end, or in SEN Workarea?

Thank you all.

entry.title: Re: Abap : Loop at bapi function module not working
entry.content:
sorry for late answer , I edit my Question with Add Code ,

Yes my problem is not going to tabx 2 and 3 ..... 

Bapi fm just retrieve the first index , second and third and .... not retrieve any data in table .

entry.title: Chart of account assign to controlling area A000

If possible change the chart of account assigned to the controlling area A000?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Issue in the integration between our customer checkout system and SAP Business One.
entry.content:
We are facing an issue with the integration between our customer checkout system and SAP Business One. We have a plugin integrated with our customer checkout system, which is designed to automatically create sales orders in SAP Business One.

The plugin itself appears to be functioning correctly, but the sales orders are not being successfully transferred to SAP Business One. Despite syncing the customer checkout , we have not been able to see any sales orders created in SAP Business One.

Additionally, we are encountering an error message that indicates the sales order document already exists in SAP Business One, but it cannot be found. This suggests there is an issue with the sync.

entry.title: Re: About SAP S/4HANA Certification.
entry.content: excellent article, congratulations.

entry.title: Re: SAP Services to find the details of company code and...
entry.content:
Hi @paule_ktpi , 

thank you for your question. Without further context as for the objective or purpose for which you are looking to get bank data associated with a company code it is not immediately clear which APIs to recommend.

A company might make its own company code data available via this API: Company Code - Read, Overview | Company Code - Read | SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

A company might make its bank connections externally available via this API: Bank - Read, Overview | Bank - Read | SAP Business Accelerator Hub. 

The company code associated with a business partner is found via the API Business_Partner_API, depending on the BP, the method for customer or supplier may have to be used:

  • Customer: method GET 
    API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_Customer('{Customer}')/to_CustomerCompany
    for example, for the SAP Sandbox in the API Business Hub you might use this URL for customer '203': https://sandbox.api.sap.com/s4hanacloud/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_Customer(%27203%27)/to_CustomerCompany (use the 'try out' function). The response yields Company Code '1710'. 
  • For Supplier, like '1018', use BP method GET 
    API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_Supplier('{Supplier}')/to_SupplierCompany
    like https://sandbox.api.sap.com/s4hanacloud/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_Supplier(%271018%27)/to_SupplierCompany. The response yields '1710' as well.

 

  • To find all banks use API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BusinessPartnerBank
  • Details of a specific bank you'd find with method /A_BusinessPartnerBank(BusinessPartner='{BusinessPartner}',BankIdentification='{BankIdentification}') with BankIdentification being a 4 digit number like '0001'

In case you have a specific journal entry available, that should give you a useful combination of some of  these data, like company code, supplier or customer business partner number.  One API to access those (if available at customer) is API Accounting Document - Read, https://api.sap.com/api/API_OPLACCTGDOCITEMCUBE_SRV

entry.title: Re: What is the size limit of parameters in parameterized...
entry.content: you may hit that limit.

Thanks.

entry.title: A connection check error occurred due to a App:communication Arrangements
entry.content:
以下の記事を参考に、拡張性アプリをカスタマイズしています。


 アプリ：通信手配の通信確認を押すとエラーが発生し、確認を進めることができません。

error1：送信サービスのpingが失敗しました

error2：403 統合接続: 禁止


エラーの原因と回避方法はこちら。

--------------------------------------------参考文献---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/extend-form-template-using-sap-s-4-hana-cloud-in-app-extensibility/ba-p/13377962

entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano

I identify as a person in the below categories:

    • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts 
    • People looking to switch their careers mid-life

I have successfully completed my scrum master Professional Certification from scrum alliance and now want to accelerate my career with SAP Digital Skills Initiatives. I am an MBA graduate.

entry.title: Hiding Story Filters or Excluding Dimension Members via Scripting in SAC
entry.content: In SAC, I want to exclude two dimension members from a table. I created a story filter to exclude these members. Is there a way to hide this story filter using scripting? Alternatively, can I directly exclude these members using scripting?

entry.title: Get $100 OFF Temu Coupon CODE{acu986007} or[acr690202] → For All Customers first orders
entry.content:
Currently, there is a FLAT $100 OFF Temu Coupon code available: {acu986007} or[acr690202]. This code can be used by Both new and Existing users for a $100 off and 70% discount on their first purchase​. Make sure to apply the code at checkout to receive the discount.

For businesses operating in Brazil, navigating the complexities of tax regulations can be a challenge. A crucial aspect of this is complying with electronic invoicing, mandated by the Nota Fiscal Eletronica (NF-e) system. This article explores SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica, a solution designed to simplify NF-e compliance for SAP users.

Understanding NF-e
NF-e refers to the electronic invoice format used in Brazil for transactions involving goods. It eliminates paper invoices, promoting tax efficiency and streamlining the invoicing process. Companies must generate NF-e in a specific XML format, containing details like buyer and seller information, product specifics, and tax data.

SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica: A Helping Hand
SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica is a software solution offered by SAP that aids businesses in complying with NF-e regulations. It integrates seamlessly with existing SAP systems, automating manual tasks and ensuring accurate invoice generation.

Key Benefits of SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica
Enhanced Compliance: The solution automates NF-e generation in the mandated XML format, minimizing errors and ensuring compliance with Brazilian tax authorities (SEFAZ).
Streamlined Processes: Manual data entry for invoices is significantly reduced, saving time and resources.
Improved Accuracy: Automating invoice creation reduces the risk of errors associated with manual data entry.
Secure Communication: Secure and certified communication with SEFAZ is facilitated through the solution.
Efficiency Gains: Faster invoice processing translates to quicker settlements and improved cash flow.
Important Note: Upcoming End of Maintenance"

It's important to be aware that SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica (SAP NFE) is an on-premise solution nearing its end-of-maintenance date (December 31, 2025). SAP recommends exploring alternative solutions to ensure continued compliance with evolving NF-e regulations.

entry.title: SAP ECC Spark to Intel migration on Solaris 10
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We are currently working on a project in Saudi Arabia. Our people are renovating a local data center and they were made aware of a very old SAP ECC 6 system. It is a full rack with two SUN Spark M5000 server nodes and a SUN StorageTek 6140 from around 2005.

The system is no longer in use but still functional. The new owner wants to secure the data and has asked us whether the physical system can be moved to a local VM or a cloud platform? The entire rack is to be removed, therefore a safe and hopefully simple migration of the system would have to be planned.

Our DBA has taken a full database export dump and also a full RMAN backup from the underlying Oracle DB system, but we have no experience in migrating the ECC application stack. Kindly note, this platform is running on Solaris 10 on Spark, but we also have virtual machines for Solaris 10 on Intel available.

Ideally the migration would be from physical Solaris 10 on Spark to virtual Solaris 10 on Intel. There are no current users working with this system, but data retention in Saudi is now 10 years and up to 15 years in special cases, therefore the system must be preserved for this purpose only.

We have a strong Oracle DB team but need someone supporting us in the application stack migration.

Thank you.

entry.content:
Currently, TEMU discount code {acr552049} or{acq977991} ◦ June 2024. This code can be used by both new and existing users to get a $100 discount on their purchase. Don't forget to apply the code at checkout to receive the discount.

 

For businesses operating in Brazil, navigating the complexities of tax regulations can be a challenge. A crucial aspect of this is complying with electronic invoicing, mandated by the Nota Fiscal Eletronica (NF-e) system. This article explores SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica, a solution designed to simplify NF-e compliance for SAP users.

Understanding NF-e
NF-e refers to the electronic invoice format used in Brazil for transactions involving goods. It eliminates paper invoices, promoting tax efficiency and streamlining the invoicing process. Companies must generate NF-e in a specific XML format, containing details like buyer and seller information, product specifics, and tax data.

SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica: A Helping Hand
SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica is a software solution offered by SAP that aids businesses in complying with NF-e regulations. It integrates seamlessly with existing SAP systems, automating manual tasks and ensuring accurate invoice generation.

Key Benefits of SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica
Enhanced Compliance: The solution automates NF-e generation in the mandated XML format, minimizing errors and ensuring compliance with Brazilian tax authorities (SEFAZ).
Streamlined Processes: Manual data entry for invoices is significantly reduced, saving time and resources.
Improved Accuracy: Automating invoice creation reduces the risk of errors associated with manual data entry.
Secure Communication: Secure and certified communication with SEFAZ is facilitated through the solution.
Efficiency Gains: Faster invoice processing translates to quicker settlements and improved cash flow.
Important Note: Upcoming End of Maintenance"

It's important to be aware that SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica (SAP NFE) is an on-premise solution nearing its end-of-maintenance date (December 31, 2025). SAP recommends exploring alternative solutions to ensure continued compliance with evolving NF-e regulations.

entry.title: Downtime-Optimized Conversion using SUM for SAP S/4HANA Conversion : InPlace ~ HANA DB vs Any DB
entry.content:
Motivation & Objective :

As most of the Customers are now looking forward to work aggressively on their S/4HANA Transformation Journey by Converting their SAP ECC systems before they are out of Maintenance on 31st December 2027 , one of the most critical challenge for many of them would be to reduce the SUM Technical Downtime. 

Over a years minimizing downtime is a key element and number# 1 priority for almost all customers while doing SAP S/4HANA Migration, specifically in a brownfield transition approach. To add complexity , customers now even look forward to take an opportunity to migrate from On Premise DC to Cloud ~ Hyperscaler under one major transformation program. 

SAP has been working continuously to help the customers to reduce the technical downtime and complete it in One single maintenance window for such a complex and challenging scenarios. 

To satisfy customer's and on their request SAP came up last year via SUM version SP17 a new GA approach - DMOVE2S4.

Below blog from our Product owner does explain in detail about this new approach

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/two-major-news-with-sum-2-0-sp-17/ba-p/13548361 

Along with Standard (~ Plain) approach, the Downtime-Optimized DMO ~ doDMO method is one such another approach which is available to customer to reduce the technical downtime requirement by migrating application tables partially during business uptime. The blog post from Boris.Rubarth will outline further technical details about Downtime-Optimized DMO.https://blogs.sap.com/2014/09/08/dmo-downtime-optimization-by-migrating-app-tables-during-uptime-pre. and SAP Note 2547309 - Downtime-optimized DMO with SUM 2.0.

Along with these 2 commonly used ones, SAP has introduced a methodology called “Downtime-Optimized Conversion” to help the customers in further reducing the technical downtime requirement for application tables for core modules like FI/CO/ML/MM-IM conversion.
This next generation approach is available for SAP customers starting with SUM 2.0 SP10 as a Pilot but last year in May'2023 with SUM version 2.0 SP17, it has been made General Available ~ GA for all the customers who can think of converting to S/4HANA and even migrating to Cloud ~ Hyperscaler or RISE . 

Please refer link for more additional details about this approach.

Project requires an educated experts that has taken the respective training ADM329 and successfully passed the Knowledge Assessment ADM329k (requires access to SAP Learning Hub).

Please also refer to this SL Toolset System Maintenance landing page for all the relevant information and for deep dive on different topics - https://support.sap.com/en/tools/software-logistics-tools.html?anchorId=section_18674764 .

Introduction on doC ~ downtime Optimized Conversion:

As compare to other available tools the major advantage of using this tool is to “perform the FI/CO/ML/MM-IM conversion of most of the data in uptime of SUM while running production operations in parallel”. Along with that field conversion for KONV and VBFA table is also moved to uptime. 

All these steps are done in downtime for other tools , so this is one big advantage of using doC and below picture speaks itself about it 

Downtime Optimized Conversion ~ doC can be used for both - source as any DB (Non-HANA DB) or InPlace conversion ~ HANA DB

 

Per my recently completed multiple project experiences of working with different customers, i will share some of the key insights related to Key comparison of phases when Source database is Any DB ~ Non-HANA DB vs HANA DB ~ InPlace conversion. 

Below tabular data is high level information of each of these phases and it's runtime will vary depending upon the size of the tables in your system. 

Downtime/UptimeSUM Phase Name- HANA DBSUM Phase Name- AnyDBDescription of the phase 
    
UptimeRUN_IOC_TOUCHRUN_SFM_TOUCH
SFM ~ Shadow Field Migration		This will handle KONV ~ KOCLU (AnyDB),VBFA and other tables related to SD Migration
    
DowntimeJOB_KONV_MigrationDelta SFM ~ Shadow Field MigrationThis will handle delta KONV ~ KOCLU (AnyDB),VBFA and other tables related to SD Migration
    
UptimeSQLRUNTASK_FDCT_TRANSFER_DIRECTEU_CLONE_CP2STAB_RUNCopy to Shadow tables happens during this time.
    
UptimeNot relevant , as tables are already on HANAEU_CLONE_MIG_OPTDMO_INI_RUNUptime table migration of the relevant tables

 

In the next blog I will share my experiences on challenges faced by customers across different execution project system cycles ,  how the doC projects should be planned for successful Project completion and which all different teams needs to be involved. 

Thank you for taking time for reading !!

-Anand Tigadikar

entry.title: Re: Using ERP Delivery Number as the EWM Document Number (ODO) in Embedded EWM
entry.content:
Hello ,

Could you please explain little more.. how it would be choose same Delivery number (ERP Ref number in inbound delivery) during outbound process?.. on what basis these two will be linked.?... ( Ex: if material have N number of deliveries in inbound and we are triggering outbound process with few quantities )

Nice explanation on synchronized delivery NR in between ECC & EWM.

Thanks you.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – SAP Fiori Security Design Fundamentals
entry.content:
Hi @Jocelyn_Dart 

I've heard from other sources within SAP that the business roles are not to be used in production environments. Is this correct from your POV? I couldn't find anything about this in your blog. To my understanding the new best practice recommendation is to create custom roles. For production use, but the business role templates can be used in development and test environments.

Kr Lisa

entry.title: Re: Welcome to Career Corner
entry.content:
Dear all,

I work for an IT recruitment agency as a strategic consultant and Scrum Master. At the same time, I am studying Computer Science B.Sc. Since the S/4HANA transformation is a recurring topic at our customers, I have become aware of the SAP world and am now looking for opportunities to become an SAP Consultant and/or SAP Developer. I am already very interested in ABAP. As I also spent 15 years in logistics management, I want to focus on supply chain solutions and technologies such as SAP (E)WM.

How can I design my learning path for this?

entry.title: TEMU discount code{aaf63818} or{act200019} -> (June, july- 2024)
entry.content:
Currently, TEMU discount code {aaf63818} or{act200019} ◦ June 2024. This code can be used by both new and existing users to get a $100 discount on their purchase. Don't forget to apply the code at checkout to receive the discount.

For businesses operating in Brazil, navigating the complexities of tax regulations can be a challenge. A crucial aspect of this is complying with electronic invoicing, mandated by the Nota Fiscal Eletronica (NF-e) system. This article explores SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica, a solution designed to simplify NF-e compliance for SAP users.

Understanding NF-e
NF-e refers to the electronic invoice format used in Brazil for transactions involving goods. It eliminates paper invoices, promoting tax efficiency and streamlining the invoicing process. Companies must generate NF-e in a specific XML format, containing details like buyer and seller information, product specifics, and tax data.

SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica: A Helping Hand
SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica is a software solution offered by SAP that aids businesses in complying with NF-e regulations. It integrates seamlessly with existing SAP systems, automating manual tasks and ensuring accurate invoice generation.

Key Benefits of SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica
Enhanced Compliance: The solution automates NF-e generation in the mandated XML format, minimizing errors and ensuring compliance with Brazilian tax authorities (SEFAZ).
Streamlined Processes: Manual data entry for invoices is significantly reduced, saving time and resources.
Improved Accuracy: Automating invoice creation reduces the risk of errors associated with manual data entry.
Secure Communication: Secure and certified communication with SEFAZ is facilitated through the solution.
Efficiency Gains: Faster invoice processing translates to quicker settlements and improved cash flow.
Important Note: Upcoming End of Maintenance"

It's important to be aware that SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica (SAP NFE) is an on-premise solution nearing its end-of-maintenance date (December 31, 2025). SAP recommends exploring alternative solutions to ensure continued compliance with evolving NF-e regulations.

entry.title: Re: Unable to find the option Write a blog
entry.content: To write a blog in the SAP Community, you need to follow a few specific steps. Firstly, you must complete the "Tour the SAP Community" tutorial. For more detailed instructions and to start the process, you can visit the following link. https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/community-2024.html

entry.title: Re: Looking to create a condition in the contract request...
entry.content:
Hi Justin,

The downstream contract request approval flow cannot be conditionalized based on the workspace amendment type field.

I would suggest you to check with our consulting team for your business requirements.

https://me.sap.com/notes/KB0394723


Regards,

Basavant

You can also earn additional Temu referral bonuses by inviting friends to join. Below are the latest active Temu referral codes available for claiming your sign-up bonus.

 


For businesses operating in Brazil, navigating the complexities of tax regulations can be a challenge. A crucial aspect of this is complying with electronic invoicing, mandated by the Nota Fiscal Eletronica (NF-e) system. This article explores SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica, a solution designed to simplify NF-e compliance for SAP users.

Understanding NF-e
NF-e refers to the electronic invoice format used in Brazil for transactions involving goods. It eliminates paper invoices, promoting tax efficiency and streamlining the invoicing process. Companies must generate NF-e in a specific XML format, containing details like buyer and seller information, product specifics, and tax data.

SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica: A Helping Hand
SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica is a software solution offered by SAP that aids businesses in complying with NF-e regulations. It integrates seamlessly with existing SAP systems, automating manual tasks and ensuring accurate invoice generation.

Key Benefits of SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica
Enhanced Compliance: The solution automates NF-e generation in the mandated XML format, minimizing errors and ensuring compliance with Brazilian tax authorities (SEFAZ).
Streamlined Processes: Manual data entry for invoices is significantly reduced, saving time and resources.
Improved Accuracy: Automating invoice creation reduces the risk of errors associated with manual data entry.
Secure Communication: Secure and certified communication with SEFAZ is facilitated through the solution.
Efficiency Gains: Faster invoice processing translates to quicker settlements and improved cash flow.
Important Note: Upcoming End of Maintenance"

It's important to be aware that SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica (SAP NFE) is an on-premise solution nearing its end-of-maintenance date (December 31, 2025). SAP recommends exploring alternative solutions to ensure continued compliance with evolving NF-e regulations.

entry.title: Re: Multiple batches for finished good boxes in a single...
entry.content:
Hi Kailas,

thanks for the clarification that helps.

From what I gather you basically want to post CO11N using a scanner and the label you are scanning is pre-printed upon release of the production order.

You actually only want one batch for your finished goods stock if possible, but you need a unique number for each box for the warehousing process (in your case storage unit). That's why you consider a batch number for each box coming out of production.

Correct? (Just to make sure my answer goes in the right direction)

Regards

Andreas

entry.title: Re: Billing and Shipping Address in SAP B1
entry.content:
Dear @deepanvip  as per your requirement follow the below mentioned steps. 

Step-by-Step Guide:

  1. Create an A/P Invoice:

    • Navigate to Purchasing - A/P > A/P Invoice.

  2. Enter Vendor Details:

    • Fill in the vendor information.

  3. Select the Billing Address:

    • In the Logistics tab, choose the correct billing address which includes the relevant GST number. This address will be used to calculate the tax.

  4. Select the Ship-To Address:

    • In the same Logistics tab, select the ship-to address where the materials will be delivered and accounted for. This address can have a different GST number.

  5. Add Items:

    • Go to the Contents tab.
    • Enter the items and quantities for the invoice.

  6. Review and Apply Tax:

    • Ensure the tax is calculated based on the GST number of the billing address.
    • Double-check that all items are being accounted for in the ship-to location.

  7. Save the A/P Invoice:

    • Review all entered information.
    • Click Add to save the invoice.

Important Points:

  • GST Configuration:

    • Ensure both locations' GST numbers are correctly set up in the system.

  • Tax Codes:

    • Verify tax codes for accuracy to ensure correct tax calculation based on the billing address.

This approach ensures the tax is calculated based on the billing address, while the materials are accounted for at the ship-to location.

entry.title: Temu Coupon Code [{acr897367} or {frg227060}] - Save $100 Today!
entry.content:
Get a $100 Temu sign-up bonus when you open an account using the latest Temu referral codes: "{acr897367}" or "{frg227060}" and make a purchase of $100 or more. You can also earn additional Temu referral bonuses by inviting friends to join. Below are the latest active Temu referral codes available for claiming your sign-up bonus.

 

For businesses operating in Brazil, navigating the complexities of tax regulations can be a challenge. A crucial aspect of this is complying with electronic invoicing, mandated by the Nota Fiscal Eletronica (NF-e) system. This article explores SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica, a solution designed to simplify NF-e compliance for SAP users.

Understanding NF-e
NF-e refers to the electronic invoice format used in Brazil for transactions involving goods. It eliminates paper invoices, promoting tax efficiency and streamlining the invoicing process. Companies must generate NF-e in a specific XML format, containing details like buyer and seller information, product specifics, and tax data.

SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica: A Helping Hand
SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica is a software solution offered by SAP that aids businesses in complying with NF-e regulations. It integrates seamlessly with existing SAP systems, automating manual tasks and ensuring accurate invoice generation.

Key Benefits of SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica
Enhanced Compliance: The solution automates NF-e generation in the mandated XML format, minimizing errors and ensuring compliance with Brazilian tax authorities (SEFAZ).
Streamlined Processes: Manual data entry for invoices is significantly reduced, saving time and resources.
Improved Accuracy: Automating invoice creation reduces the risk of errors associated with manual data entry.
Secure Communication: Secure and certified communication with SEFAZ is facilitated through the solution.
Efficiency Gains: Faster invoice processing translates to quicker settlements and improved cash flow.
Important Note: Upcoming End of Maintenance"

It's important to be aware that SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica (SAP NFE) is an on-premise solution nearing its end-of-maintenance date (December 31, 2025). SAP recommends exploring alternative solutions to ensure continued compliance with evolving NF-e regulations.

For businesses operating in Brazil, navigating the complexities of tax regulations can be a challenge. A crucial aspect of this is complying with electronic invoicing, mandated by the Nota Fiscal Eletronica (NF-e) system. This article explores SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica, a solution designed to simplify NF-e compliance for SAP users.

Understanding NF-e
NF-e refers to the electronic invoice format used in Brazil for transactions involving goods. It eliminates paper invoices, promoting tax efficiency and streamlining the invoicing process. Companies must generate NF-e in a specific XML format, containing details like buyer and seller information, product specifics, and tax data.

SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica: A Helping Hand
SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica is a software solution offered by SAP that aids businesses in complying with NF-e regulations. It integrates seamlessly with existing SAP systems, automating manual tasks and ensuring accurate invoice generation.

Key Benefits of SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica
Enhanced Compliance: The solution automates NF-e generation in the mandated XML format, minimizing errors and ensuring compliance with Brazilian tax authorities (SEFAZ).
Streamlined Processes: Manual data entry for invoices is significantly reduced, saving time and resources.
Improved Accuracy: Automating invoice creation reduces the risk of errors associated with manual data entry.
Secure Communication: Secure and certified communication with SEFAZ is facilitated through the solution.
Efficiency Gains: Faster invoice processing translates to quicker settlements and improved cash flow.
Important Note: Upcoming End of Maintenance"

It's important to be aware that SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica (SAP NFE) is an on-premise solution nearing its end-of-maintenance date (December 31, 2025). SAP recommends exploring alternative solutions to ensure continued compliance with evolving NF-e regulations.

entry.title: BingX Referral Code Is "WVXHLW" - How to Sign Up BingX in 2024
entry.content: Ways to Get the Most Out of Your Workout, According to Research.

entry.title: Re: SAP REPORT QUERY
entry.content:
Dear @KULBEERSINGH as per your requirement.

SELECT
T0.DocNum AS [Sales Order No.],
T0.DocDate AS [Sales Order Date],
T0.CardCode AS [Customer Code],
T1.CardName AS [Customer Name],
T2.DocNum AS [AR Invoice No.],
T2.DocDate AS [AR Invoice Date],
DATEDIFF(day, T0.DocDate, T2.DocDate) AS [Gap Days]
FROM
ORDR T0
LEFT JOIN
OCRD T1 ON T0.CardCode = T1.CardCode
LEFT JOIN
OINV T2 ON T0.DocEntry = T2.BaseEntry AND T2.ObjType = 13
WHERE
T0.DocStatus = 'C' -- 'C' means Closed (fully delivered and invoiced)
ORDER BY
T0.DocNum;
 

 

Regards,
Faraz Maqsud

entry.title: Re: Analysis of integration requirements for Advanced Fin...
entry.content:
Hi, please check online help of AFC https://help.sap.com/docs/advanced-financial-closing/administration/connectivity?locale=en-US and select the system you have. For SAP ERP systems it is a REST service and for SAP S/4HANA systems it is a ODATA service.

For certificates please see the Cloud Trust Center and select SAP BTP. As SAP AFC is running on top of SAP BTP you will find the relevant certificates under SAP BTP.

Regards

Thomas

entry.title: Re: Appending existing standard CDS view with CDC annotation
entry.content: Thanks BR, Martin for quick reply. I tried to create custom CDS view on top of I_Actualplanjournalentryitem standard view but getting error: Namespace of DDL 'ZI_ACTUALPLANJOURNALENTRYITEM' does not match namespace of VIEWNAME 'IFIACTPLNJEICSQL'. I have not used any CDC annotation yet. Any clue.

entry.title: Re: Let`s get down to nitty-gritty of "Manage Workflows for Maintenance Orders"-S4 HANA As
entry.content:
Dear Nick,

Currently, I have a custom report to get the decision note & comment for both rejection and approval of the work order...for instance, I have a WO 4000225 , it was initially rejected with comment & decision note and then post re-submission of the work order, I approved it...

Initial rejection with comment and decision note...

after re-submission, it was approved.

the Decision note and Comment provided by an approver can be retrieved from below tables...

Mini Note: Once we approve or reject the work order with decision note/comment, system updates it as an office document in the workflow item...

--

Sincerely,

Pardha Reddy.C

Kind regards

entry.title: Re: Why do PowerDesigner XML files contain non-ASCII char...
entry.content:
Hi George,

your model seems to be called LDM_DATA_MODEL_BIN. Are you sure you haven`t saved it in binary format? That would be pretty good explanation for binary characters presence in the model...

If binary characters are present even in the XML format of the model, that would indeed be quite strange. The only scenario I can think of is manual insertion (copy-paste) directly into the file, meaning neither using the PD GUI nor the PD API.

Regards,

Ondrej Divis

entry.title: How can I restrict the V2oDataModel automatic MERGE call on cell value change in SAPUI5 grid table?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am using V2 ODataModel(sap.ui.model.odata.v2.ODataModel) for performing list binding to a grid table(sap.ui.table.Table) in my SAPUI5 application.

There are a few editable columns in the grid table. When I try to change any value in the editable column cell, it automatically triggers oData MERGE call.

Editable fields in Grid table:

Immediate oData MERGE call:

Is there any way to stop this automatic MERGE call? And I also want to store the modified data(which is working) of editable cells in v2 oDataModel which is required to send the modified entries data to the S4HANA.

entry.title: Re: Store attachments from Standard Fiori apps in Archive...
entry.content:
Dear,

1st of all it is important to understand that ArchiveLink technology is being replaced by DMS in the Fiori Apps.

HDM - Harmonized document management - is the global term on top of the DMS and KPro technologies.

ArchiveLink attachments can in some cases still be displayed in some Fiori Apps, but not in all cases, and such attachments are not supposed to be created anymore in S/4.

SAP is in a kind of transition phase where, when you add attachments, for some Fiori Apps they are still stored as GOS (KPro), and for some others as DMS.

The use of DMS allows to have distinct document types for different attachments when needed, as it was possible in ArchiveLink.

So in practice there is very often a "discrepancy" between what you can see in usual SAPGUI/WebGUI (= Fioritized) transactions (usually ArchiveLink and GOS/Kpro) and what you can see in Fiori Apps (usually GOS/KPro and DMS).

For cases where there is a business need to use both WebGUI (as Fioritized) and Fiori Apps, and there are such cases, then the only option is to put in place an attachment-link-sync applications that ensures that attachments are made visible in all different types of UI. 

You may either try to develop such an application -not always obvious- or use the one on the market, YAC by ZNK.

entry.title: Bin Structure and Template
entry.content:
I trying to create Bin structure and template but ending up in error like "Storage bin must not begin with a blank character" or template not correct.

I have the below Storage Type with  bins  Aisle is 01, Stack is A1 and A,B,C are bin level

01A1A
01A1B
01A1C

01B1B

01C1C

02A1B
02A1C

02C1A

02C1C
02D1A
02D1B
02D1C
02E1A
02E1B
02E1C
02G1A
02G1B
02G1C

Could you please help me in Bin structure and Template.

Regards,

Chand Basha.S

entry.title: SAP Gateway Error Log in Fiori apps Run Settlement - Actual
entry.content:
Hello,

I encounter an issue where there is no Processing Type in the Run Settlement - Actual when pressing the button "Run"/"Test Run". 

It should show like below:

 

I have go through the Troubleshoot RAP since the apps is using RAP and by using the ADT feed reader, I managed to get the Gateway Error Log: Entity 'C_RUNSETTLMTACTLPROCESSINGTYPE': Parameter entity set 'C_RunSettlmtActlProcessingType' cannot be used for read. However, I have no idea how to solve the issue as the CDS view C_RUNSETTLMTACTLPROCESSINGTYPE is a custom entity and use an ABAP class for the data retrieval. Upon debugging, the class is not triggered and I stuck on how to solve this. How should I solve this? Thank you.

SAP BTP, ABAP environment ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model
